title: Tofu Stir-Fry Bowl

subs.name: paneer (cubed) for a dairy-based high-protein alternative — not vegan
cauliflower, mushrooms or green beans — same stir-fry time

ingredients.name: spring onion, sliced, whites and greens separated

subs.name: tamari (gluten-free soy sauce) for a gluten-free version

steps.text: Press the tofu firmly between two plates lined with kitchen cloth for 10 minutes to expel moisture — the drier the tofu, the crispier it will fry. Cut into 2 cm cubes and dust lightly with cornflour if using, which adds crunch.
Cook the rice in 1.75 cups water until tender and fluffy. Keep warm.

Heat 1.5 tbsp oil in a wok on high flame until shimmering. Add tofu cubes in a single layer and fry without stirring for 3-4 minutes until the base is deep golden, then turn and fry remaining sides 2-3 minutes. Remove to a plate.

Add remaining oil to the wok and stir-fry the spring onion whites, garlic and ginger for 30 seconds. Add broccoli and capsicum and stir-fry on high for 3-4 minutes until the broccoli is bright green with charred edges but still has a crisp bite.

Return the tofu to the wok, pour in soy sauce and black pepper, and toss everything together on high for 1 minute until the sauce coats the tofu and vegetables evenly. Serve immediately over rice, topped with spring onion greens.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: tofu → paneer (cubed) for a dairy-based high-protein alternative — not vegan; soy-sauce → tamari (gluten-free soy sauce) for a gluten-free version; broccoli → cauliflower, mushrooms or green beans — same stir-fry time
indian_kitchen_adaptation: A kadai works perfectly in place of a wok — use it on the highest possible flame. Tofu (called 'soya paneer' in some Indian stores) is now available in most supermarkets and Amazon. Replace soy sauce with 1 tbsp of ginger-garlic paste plus a dash of vinegar if soy is unavailable — it is not authentic but keeps the protein content identical.
